description: Steel erection procedures — erection sequence, column setting, beam connections, bolt-up methods, plumbing and aligning, equipment setting for vessels and exchangers, pipe rack erection, and field welding of structural connections.

erection_sequence.general_rule: Erect from the center outward or from the braced bay outward. Complete connections and bracing in each bay before moving to the next. Never leave a partially connected bay overnight without temporary bracing.
erection_sequence.columns_first: Set columns, install minimum 4 anchor bolts snug-tight, plumb within tolerance, install temporary bracing if needed before releasing crane.
erection_sequence.then_beams: Hang beams between columns. Minimum 2 bolts at each connection before releasing crane. Complete bolt-up of each connection before loading with additional steel.
erection_sequence.then_bracing: Install diagonal bracing per erection drawings. Bracing provides lateral stability — without it, the frame is unstable.
erection_sequence.then_decking: Install metal decking as soon as practical behind erection. Decking acts as a diaphragm and provides fall protection.
erection_sequence.then_misc: Grating, handrail, stairs, toe boards, checkered plate after primary steel and decking.

methods.leveling_nuts: Heavy hex nuts on anchor bolts below base plate. Level plate by adjusting nuts. MOST COMMON for industrial columns.
methods.shim_stacks: Stainless steel shim packs at 3 or 4 points around base plate.
methods.leveling_screws: Jacking bolts in base plate legs. Fine adjustment.

base_plate_leveling.tolerance: Level within 1/16 inch across base plate for most industrial columns. Tighter for tall columns (plumbness accumulates).

column_setting.anchor_bolt_tightening: After column is plumbed, tighten anchor bolts to snug-tight minimum. For base plates designed as moment connections, pretension anchor bolts per engineer's spec.
column_setting.grouting: After column is plumbed, aligned, and base plate bolt-up complete — grout under base plate. Non-shrink cementitious or epoxy grout. Fill completely — no voids.

plumbing.tolerance: AISC Code of Standard Practice: 1/500 of column height, max 1 inch toward building exterior, max 2 inches total.
plumbing.methods: Transit or total station sighting two faces. Plumb bob for short columns. Laser plumb for tall columns.
plumbing.correction: Adjust by shimming base plate, adjusting leveling nuts, or using turnbuckle on temporary bracing cable. NEVER heat or bend column to correct plumbness.

shear_connections.types: Single plate (shear tab), double angle, single angle, seated connection, end plate.
shear_connections.bolt_up: Install all bolts in connection. Snug-tight for bearing-type, pretensioned for slip-critical. Sequence: start from center of bolt group, work outward.
shear_connections.erection_bolts: Minimum 2 bolts per OSHA before releasing crane. Then complete remaining bolts before loading connection with additional steel.

moment_connections.description: Connection designed to transfer moment (bending) from beam to column. Full penetration welds on beam flanges, bolted or welded web connection.
moment_connections.CJP_welds: Complete Joint Penetration welds on flanges. Requires backing bar, weld access holes in beam web, and UT inspection.
moment_connections.quality: Moment connections are structural critical. All CJP welds must be UT inspected per AWS D1.1. Filler metal must match or exceed base metal strength.
moment_connections.seismic: In seismic zones, moment connections have special requirements — reduced beam section (dogbone), prequalified connections per AISC 358. Welding is even more critical.

field_welding.AWS_D1_1: Structural Welding Code — Steel. Governs ALL structural welding.
field_welding.WPS_required: Every structural weld must be made per a qualified WPS (Welding Procedure Specification).
field_welding.welder_qualification: Every welder must be qualified per AWS D1.1 for the position and process being used. Qualification records on file.
field_welding.preheat: Required for thick material (over 3/4 inch for A36, over 1 inch for A992) and for low ambient temperature (below 32F, preheat to 70F minimum for any thickness).
field_welding.inspection: Visual (VT) on all welds. UT on CJP welds per code. MT or PT on fillet welds if required by specification.
field_welding.wind_protection: Shield welding from wind. FCAW-GS: max 5 mph at arc. SMAW: more tolerant but still shield. FCAW-SS: most wind-tolerant field process.

vessels.vertical_vessels: Tailed up from horizontal to vertical using crane and tail crane. Tailing lug and lifting lugs per manufacturer rigging diagram. CRITICAL LIFT.
vessels.horizontal_vessels: Set on saddles. Level on saddle centerlines. Allow for thermal growth — one saddle is fixed, other has slotted holes.
vessels.orientation: Verify nozzle orientation matches P&ID and plot plan. Rotating a vessel after setting is extremely costly.
vessels.anchor_bolts: Install and hand-tight only until vessel is aligned. Then tighten per specification.

heat_exchangers.setting: Set on support saddles or structural steel frame. Level across tube sheet faces.
heat_exchangers.bundle_pull_space: Verify there is enough clearance on the tube bundle pull end for bundle removal. Common design oversight — structure blocks bundle removal.
heat_exchangers.nozzle_alignment: Verify nozzle orientation and elevation match piping design before tightening hold-downs.

columns_towers.tailing_up: Large columns/towers are shipped horizontal, tailed up in field. Requires detailed rigging plan — CG shifts during tilt.
columns_towers.internals: Some columns have internals (trays, packing) installed after erection. Some are shipped with internals installed. Verify before rigging — internals add weight and change CG.
columns_towers.davit: Davit arm on column head for lifting internals, manway covers, instruments during maintenance.

pipe_racks.erection: Columns set first (usually pre-assembled with stub-up for beam connection). Then transverse beams. Then longitudinal struts and bracing.
pipe_racks.alignment: Survey centerline and elevation of all columns. Adjust before completing connections.
pipe_racks.typical_tolerances: Column spacing: +/- 1/4 inch. Beam elevation: +/- 1/4 inch. Column plumbness: 1/500 of height.

snug_tight.definition: Plies of joint in firm contact. Achieved by a few impacts of impact wrench or full effort of one person with a standard wrench.
snug_tight.use: Bearing-type connections where slip is acceptable. Most shear connections in industrial structures.

methods.turn_of_nut: Snug all bolts, then apply additional rotation. 1/3 turn for bolt up to 4D long, 1/2 turn for 4D-8D, 2/3 turn for 8D-12D. Mark with crayon to track rotation.
methods.calibrated_wrench: Calibrate wrench with Skidmore-Wilhelm using bolts from the actual lot being installed. Daily calibration required.
methods.TC_bolt: Shear wrench until spline shears. Simplest field method.
methods.DTI: Tighten until DTI washer gap meets specification (0.005 inch typical with feeler gauge).

inspection.visual: All bolts in place, no missing bolts, nuts flush with bolt end, washers properly placed.
inspection.snug_tight_check: Arbitrarily selected bolts checked with wrench — should not turn further.

pretensioned_check.turn_of_nut: Verify crayon marks show correct rotation from snug.
pretensioned_check.DTI: Feeler gauge check on 10% of bolts minimum.
